subject: Science
title: CBSE Class 10 Science — Sample Question Paper 2024-25
_provenance: Every prompt + option + msAnswer transcribed VERBATIM from the official CBSE 2024-25 Science SQP and Marking Scheme (D-120: retrieve, don't generate). 'For Visually Impaired' alternates are included as variant 'VI' rows — a student encounters them, so they are part of the set.
labNote: Lab/practical questions are asked IN FULL and GRADED like every other question — never acknowledged-and-skipped (Jace 2026-06-10). If it is in the exam it has a correct answer, so a wrong/blank answer is a real weakness: it routes the learner into concept remediation (director finds the weak node(s) → teleport through each lesson node, deepening if still stuck → re-pose the original question). CBSE board 'experiment' questions are DESCRIBE/EXPLAIN type, fully gradeable against the marking scheme; there is no perform-the-experiment escape hatch.

contract.note: ~33% internal choice; many questions also carry a 'For Visually Impaired' alternate (variant VI).

questions.prompt: Identify 'p', 'q' and 'r' in the following balanced reaction: p Pb(NO3)2(s) --Heat--> q PbO(s) + r NO2(g) + O2(g)

Match Column I (a: metal that forms amphoteric oxides; b: metal which melts when kept on our palm; c: metal that reacts with nitric acid; d: metal which cannot displace hydrogen from acids) with Column II (i: Ga, ii: Au, iii: Al, iv: Mn) and select the correct option.
questions.correct: a–(iii), b–(i), c–(iv), d–(ii)
questions.msAnswer: B. a – (iii), b – (i), c – (iv), d – (ii)

questions.prompt: The solution in the given figure (litmus/conductivity test) is likely to be
questions.correct: C2H5OH
questions.msAnswer: A. C2H5OH

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) Which among the following is considered as the strongest electrolyte?
questions.correct: Dilute acid
questions.msAnswer: A. Dilute acid

questions.prompt: An aqueous solution 'A' turns phenolphthalein pink. On addition of aqueous solution 'B' to 'A', the pink colour disappears. Which statement is true for 'A' and 'B'?
options: A is strongly basic and B is a weak base.
A is strongly acidic and B is a weak acid.
A has a pH greater than 7 and B has a pH less than 7.
A has a pH less than 7 and B has a pH greater than 7.
questions.correct: A has a pH greater than 7 and B has a pH less than 7.
questions.msAnswer: C. A has a pH greater than 7 and B has a pH less than 7

questions.prompt: When 50g of lead powder is added to 300 ml of blue copper sulphate solution, after a few hours the solution becomes colourless. This is an example of
questions.correct: Displacement reaction
questions.msAnswer: C. Displacement reaction

questions.prompt: The electronic configuration of three elements X, Y and Z are X- 2,8,7; Y- 2,8,2; and Z- 2,8.
options: X is a non-metal and Y is a metal
Y is a non-metal and Z is a metal
questions.correct: X is a non-metal and Y is a metal
questions.msAnswer: C. X is a non-metal and Y is a metal.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an endothermic reaction?
questions.correct: Cooking of food.
questions.msAnswer: B. Cooking of food

questions.prompt: During cellular oxidation of Glucose, which event is associated with production of maximum ATP molecules per molecule of Glucose? Synthesis of
questions.correct: carbon dioxide in human cells
questions.msAnswer: D. carbon dioxide in human cells

questions.prompt: During which stage of circulation of blood in a normal human being is the oxygenated blood pumped to all parts of the body?
questions.correct: contraction of left ventricle
questions.msAnswer: B. contraction of left ventricle.

questions.prompt: Which of the following adaptations in herbivores helps in digestion of cellulose?
questions.correct: Longer small intestine
questions.msAnswer: D. Longer small intestine.

questions.prompt: There was a cerebellar dysfunction in a patient. Which of the following activities will get disturbed as a result?
questions.correct: Posture and balance
questions.msAnswer: C. Posture and balance.

questions.prompt: In snails, individuals can begin life as male and depending on environmental conditions can become female as they grow. This is because
options: male snails have dominant genetic makeup.
female snails have dominant genetic makeup.
expression of sex chromosomes can change in a snail's life time.
sex is not genetically determined in snails.
questions.correct: sex is not genetically determined in snails.
questions.msAnswer: D. sex is not genetically determined in snails.

questions.prompt: A ray is incident on a concave mirror. In which case is the angle of incidence equal to zero?
options: A ray parallel to the principal axis.
A ray passing through the centre of curvature and incident obliquely.
A ray passing through the principal focus and incident obliquely.
A ray incident obliquely to the principal axis, at the pole of the mirror.
questions.correct: A ray passing through the centre of curvature and incident obliquely.
questions.msAnswer: B. A ray passing through the centre of curvature and incident obliquely.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct option for the colour of rays for A and B (dispersion of white light through a prism).
options: Ray A: Blue, Ray B: Red
Ray A: Green, Ray B: Yellow
Ray A: Red, Ray B: Violet
Ray A: Violet, Ray B: Indigo
questions.correct: Ray A: Red, Ray B: Violet
questions.msAnswer: C. Red  Violet

questions.prompt: Identify the incorrect statement. 'The energy available to the producers is maximum' because:
options: It is the first trophic level which absorbs 1% of light energy directly from the source.
It utilizes the most of the chemical energy for its own respiration, growth, reproduction, movement etc.
It utilizes 10% of light energy and transfers the rest to the next trophic level.
It transfers only 10% of light energy to the next trophic level.
questions.correct: It utilizes 10% of light energy and transfers the rest to the next trophic level.
questions.msAnswer: C. It utilizes 10% of light energy and transfers the rest to the next trophic level.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a role of decomposers in the ecosystem?
questions.correct: Enrichment of oxygen in atmosphere.
questions.msAnswer: B. Enrichment of oxygen in the atmosphere.

questions.prompt: Assertion (A): On adding dil. HCl to a test tube containing a substance 'X', a colourless gas is produced which gives a pop sound when a burning match stick is brought near it. Reason (R): In this reaction metal 'X' is displaced by Hydrogen.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, and R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
questions.correct: A is true but R is false.
questions.msAnswer: C. A is true but R is false

questions.prompt: Assertion (A): Generally, the number of chromosomes in a cell and in a germ cell is not the same in species. Reason (R): When two germ cells combine, they restore the normal number of chromosomes in a species.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, and R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
questions.correct: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
questions.msAnswer: A. Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.

questions.prompt: Assertion (A): A convex mirror always forms an image behind it and the image formed is virtual. Reason (R): According to the sign convention, the focal length of a convex mirror is positive.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, and R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
questions.correct: Both A and R are true, and R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.msAnswer: B. Both A and R are true, and R is not the correct explanation of A

questions.prompt: Assertion (A): If the lions are removed from a food chain it will not affect the food chain, however if the plants are removed it will disturb the ecosystem. Reason (R): Plants are producers who can make food using sunlight, while lions are consumers.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, and R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
questions.correct: A is false but R is true.
questions.msAnswer: D. A is false but R is true.

questions.prompt: Identify the type of each of the following reactions stating the reason: A. Fe2O3 + 2Al → Al2O3 + 2Fe + heat. B. Pb(NO3)2 + 2KI → PbI2 + 2KNO3.
questions.msAnswer: A. Exothermic Displacement/Redox reaction — heat is evolved / a more reactive element (Al) displaces a less reactive element (reduces Fe2O3 to Fe). B. Double displacement / Precipitation reaction — exchange of ions; yellow precipitate of lead iodide forms.

questions.prompt: Differentiate between alveoli and nephron on the basis of (1) Structure and location, (2) Function.
questions.msAnswer: Alveoli: balloon-like structures at the terminal ends of bronchioles in lungs; function = exchange of gases. Nephron: tubular structure present in kidneys; function = filtration of blood to form urine.

questions.prompt: List the steps for the synthesis of glucose by plants. What special feature is found in desert plants related to this process?
questions.msAnswer: Absorption of light energy by chlorophyll; conversion to chemical energy and splitting of water into H and O; reduction of CO2 into carbohydrates. Desert plants take up CO2 at night and form an intermediate acted upon by light-energy during the day.

questions.prompt: Explain the role of the following enzymes in digestion of food in humans: (i) Salivary amylase (ii) Pepsin (iii) Trypsin (iv) Lipase.
questions.msAnswer: Salivary amylase — breaks down starch to sugar. Pepsin — digests proteins in the stomach. Trypsin — digests proteins to amino acids. Lipase — breaks down emulsified fats to fatty acids and glycerol.

questions.prompt: A figure shows image formation by a lens. A. What is the type of lens used? B. What is the nature of the image? C. If the image is formed at 30 cm from the lens and is twice the size of the object, where is the object placed?
questions.msAnswer: A. Convex lens. B. Virtual image. C. m = v/u = hi/ho = 2 ⟹ −30/u = 2 ⟹ u = −15 cm.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) A. What type of lens always forms a virtual, erect and diminished image? B. List two uses of such a lens.
questions.msAnswer: A. Concave lens. B. Correction of myopia; peepholes of doors (or any other correct use).

questions.prompt: A clothes dryer circuit has two heaters X and Y in parallel across a 230 V supply. When both switches are closed, the current in X is 3.5 A. (i) Calculate the power developed in heater X. (ii) If the resistance of X is double that of Y, calculate the current in heater Y.
questions.msAnswer: (i) P = VI = 230 × 3.5 = 805 W. (ii) I ∝ 1/R, so half the resistance ⟹ double the current ⟹ current in Y = 7.0 A.

questions.prompt: Two resistors X and Y connected in series to a battery dissipate power P1. When connected in parallel to the same battery the power dissipated is P2. Find the ratio P1/P2. (Given X = 2Y)
questions.msAnswer: Series: R+2R = 3R, P1 = V²/3R. Parallel: 2R/3, P2 = 3V²/2R. P1/P2 = 2/9.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) We have four resistors A, B, C and D of resistance 3 Ω, 6 Ω, 9 Ω and 12 Ω respectively. Find the lowest resistance which can be obtained by combining these four resistors.
questions.msAnswer: Lowest when all in parallel: 1/Req = 1/3 + 1/6 + 1/9 + 1/12 = 24/36 = 2/3 ⟹ Req = 1.5 Ω.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) You are given two fuse wires A and B with current ratings 2A and 5A respectively. Justify which one you would use with a 1000W, 220V room heater.
questions.msAnswer: I = P/V = 1000/220 = 4.54 A. Use fuse B (5A) — it sustains 4.54 A; fuse A (2A) would melt as the current exceeds its rating.

questions.prompt: A cartoon addresses a growing concern (pesticide accumulation). What impact will the process shown have on humans if they occupy the last trophic level? Explain.
questions.msAnswer: Pesticide is non-biodegradable / accumulates at each trophic level / persists for long; the last trophic level (humans) has the highest concentration — biological magnification (any 2 points).

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) Create a food chain with more than 2 trophic levels that exists in a cabbage farm. If humans occupy the last trophic level, how would spraying pesticide affect the humans? Explain.
questions.msAnswer: e.g. Cabbage/plant → rabbit → snake → owl. Pesticides are non-biodegradable and persist; they accumulate at each trophic level (biological magnification), so humans at the last level get the highest concentration.

questions.prompt: A. Anirudh has two unlabelled metal oxides — aluminium oxide and magnesium oxide. How will you guide him to identify and label them? B. Aishu obtained copper from Cu2S and Cu2O. Illustrate with chemical equations how she might have done it.
questions.msAnswer: A. Aqueous magnesium oxide turns red litmus blue; aluminium oxide is amphoteric and insoluble, so changes neither litmus (or: MgO reacts with acid only, Al2O3 reacts with both acids and bases). B. 2Cu2S + 3O2 → 2Cu2O + 2SO2; 2Cu2O + Cu2S → 6Cu + SO2 (self-reduction).

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) Give reasons: (i) Certain metals are used for making cooking utensils. (ii) Hydrogen gas does not evolve when certain metals (except Mg & Mn) react with nitric acid.
questions.msAnswer: (i) Metals like aluminium/copper are good conductors of heat with high melting points. (ii) HNO3 is a strong oxidising agent; it oxidises the H2 produced to water while itself being reduced to nitrogen oxides. Only Mg and Mn (with very dilute HNO3) evolve H2.

questions.prompt: (i) In the given series of reactions, name compounds X and Z. (ii) Which type of reaction is X to Z? (iii) Three solutions A, B, C have pH 6, 8 and 9.5. In which is the maximum number of hydronium ions present? Arrange in increasing order of H+ concentration.
questions.msAnswer: (i) X = NaHCO3, Z = Na2CO3. (ii) Decomposition reaction. (iii) Solution A (lowest pH) has most H+; increasing order of H+: C < B < A.

questions.prompt: Comment on: (i) Bee sting is treated with baking soda paste whereas wasp sting is treated with dilute vinegar. (ii) Farmers treat soil with quicklime when tilling. (iii) Ancient sculptures and marble structures are conserved by treating them with certain chemicals.
questions.msAnswer: (i) Bee sting is acidic (neutralised by basic baking soda); wasp sting is basic (neutralised by acidic vinegar). (ii) To change the nature of acidic soil to neutral or basic. (iii) To protect sculptures from acidic gases in the environment and acid rain.

questions.prompt: Water is used by leaves for photosynthesis, but we water the plant through the soil. How does this water reach the leaves of the plant?
questions.msAnswer: Water is absorbed from soil by roots; xylem of roots, stems and leaves forms a continuous channel. During the day, with stomata open, transpiration pull is the major driving force; evaporation from stomata creates a suction pulling water up the xylem from roots to leaves.

questions.prompt: A. Father has long ears (pure dominant) and mother short ears (recessive). If an F1 individual marries an unrelated individual of the same genotype, calculate the ratio of genetic makeup of F2. Show a suitable cross. B. If father had short ears and mother long ears, both homozygous, what effect on the F2 genetic-makeup ratio (F1 × same-genotype individual)?
questions.msAnswer: A. LL × ll ⟹ F1 = Ll. Ll × Ll ⟹ 1LL : 2Ll : 1ll. B. No change — the ratio is still 1LL : 2Ll : 1ll, as the cross is still between a pure dominant and recessive.

questions.prompt: A. What is the fundamental difference between hypermetropia and myopia in terms of the optical experience of a person? B. A diagram shows a special eye defect: (i) What is the defect? (ii) State one cause. (iii) Explain with reason whether a concave lens can correct it.
questions.msAnswer: A. Hypermetropia — difficulty focusing on near objects, clearer for distant. Myopia — distant objects blurry, near clear. B. (i) Hypermetropia. (ii) Shortening of the eyeball / focal length of eye lens too long. (iii) No — a concave lens would diverge the rays and push the image further; a convex lens is needed to converge rays onto the retina.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) A. What is the fundamental difference between hypermetropia and myopia in terms of the optical experience of a person? B. What are the causes of myopia in the human eye?
questions.msAnswer: A. Hypermetropia — difficulty focusing on near objects, clearer for distant; Myopia — distant blurry, near clear. B. High converging power of the eye lens; elongation of the eyeball.

questions.prompt: A. State the relationship between the resistance R of a wire and its length l and cross-sectional area A, using mathematical symbols to arrive at the final formula. B. Using the formula, define the resistivity of a material.
questions.msAnswer: A. R ∝ l, R ∝ 1/A ⟹ R = ρ l/A. B. ρ = RA/l; for A = 1 m² and l = 1 m, ρ = R — resistivity is the resistance offered by a wire of length 1 m and cross-sectional area 1 m².

questions.prompt: Mona moved a magnetic compass away from a straight current-carrying wire and the deflection reduced. A. Explain why the deflection reduced. B. Mention one change in the circuit that could increase the deflection. C. Explain with reason the direction of the magnetic field for the case in the figure.
questions.msAnswer: A. Magnetic field strength is inversely proportional to distance from the wire, so moving away reduces the effect and the deflection. B. Increase the current in the circuit (field ∝ current). C. With current going from top to bottom of the plane, by the right-hand thumb rule the magnetic field is clockwise.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) A. Explain why the deflection of the compass needle reduced as Mona moved away from the current-carrying wire. B. Mention one change that could increase the deflection. C. Explain with reason how the direction of the magnetic field changes if the polarity of the battery is reversed.
questions.msAnswer: A. Field strength ∝ 1/distance, so deflection reduces with distance. B. Increase the current. C. Reversing the battery polarity reverses the current direction and hence reverses the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.prompt: (i) Keerthi thinks substitution reactions occur in saturated hydrocarbons; Krishi thinks in unsaturated. Justify whose thinking is correct. (ii) 'Methane and Propane and their isomers are used as fuels' — comment. Draw the electron-dot structure of the immediate lower homologue of propane. Give any two characteristics of homologues. (iii) A mixture of oxygen and ethyne is burnt for welding — why is a mixture of ethyne and air not used?
questions.msAnswer: (i) Keerthi is correct: substitution occurs in saturated hydrocarbons (H replaced by heteroatoms); unsaturated undergo addition across double/triple bonds. (ii) Methane and propane undergo combustion in oxygen producing large energy; lower homologue of propane is ethane (C2H6); characteristics: successive members differ by –CH2–/14u, same general formula/functional group, similar chemical properties, gradual change in physical properties. (iii) Ethyne+oxygen gives complete combustion (clean blue flame for welding); with air (insufficient O2) it burns incompletely with a sooty flame.

questions.prompt: A and B are sodium salts of long-chain carboxylic acid and long-chain sulphonic acid respectively. (i) Which would you prefer as a cleansing agent with underground (hard) water? Give the reason. (ii) Elaborate on the process of cleansing action; illustrate a micelle with a labelled diagram. (iii) Write the chemical equation for preparing soap from the ester CH3COOCH3 and name the process.
questions.msAnswer: (i) B (sulphonic acid salt) — Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺ salts of sulphonic acid are soluble (carboxylic-acid salts precipitate in hard water). (ii) Soap molecules have a hydrophilic ionic end and a hydrophobic carbon end; in water they form micelles (carbon ends toward oil/dirt, ionic ends out) forming an emulsion that washes dirt away. (iii) CH3(CH2)16COOR + NaOH → CH3(CH2)16COONa + ROH; the process is saponification.

questions.prompt: A banana plant grows with the help of suckers. (i) Give the name and type of reproduction shown. (ii) List two advantages the farmer has using this type of reproduction. (iii) The plant produces male flowers — explain how it will be involved in pollination. (iv) Why is the offspring not absolutely identical to its parent plant?
questions.msAnswer: (i) Vegetative propagation / asexual reproduction. (ii) More crops in the same time, genetically identical, faster flowering/fruiting, no dependence on pollinators. (iii) Cross pollination — pollen from the anther is transferred to the stigma of another banana plant by wind/water/insects. (iv) Minor variations occur during the copying of DNA.

questions.prompt: A developing fetus is connected to the placenta by the umbilical cord (umbilical vein and artery run inside it). (i) Name two substances that move through the blood vessels. (ii) If the placenta has fewer villi, how will it affect the baby's growth? (iii) Name the region where the embryo develops and explain how it is adapted for nourishing the baby. (iv) Fetal cells screened from amniotic fluid contain XY chromosomes: a) What is the sex? b) How is prenatal sex determination misused?
questions.msAnswer: (i) Nutrients/glucose/oxygen/waste (any two). (ii) Less surface area for nutrients to pass mother→embryo ⟹ slow growth. (iii) Uterus; it has a thick lining with a rich blood supply to nourish the embryo. (iv) a) Male child. b) Misused for female foeticide — aborting female foetuses.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) A developing fetus is connected to the placenta by the umbilical cord. (i) Name two substances that move through the blood vessels. (ii) If the placenta has fewer villi, how will it affect growth? (iii) Name the region where the embryo develops and how it is adapted. (iv) Fetal cells contain XY chromosomes: a) sex? b) how is prenatal sex determination misused?
questions.msAnswer: (i) Nutrients/glucose/oxygen/waste (any two). (ii) Less surface area ⟹ slow growth. (iii) Uterus; thick lining, rich blood supply. (iv) a) Male child. b) Female foeticide.

questions.prompt: A circuit (part of an electrical device): R2 is in parallel with a 4 Ω resistor (current 1.5 A through the 4 Ω); total current through the parallel pair is 2.0 A; a 2.0 Ω resistor and R1 are in series; total p.d. is 12 V. Calculate: (i) Potential difference across R2. (ii) Value of R2. (iii) Value of R1.
questions.msAnswer: (i) p.d. across R2 = p.d. across 4 Ω (parallel) = 1.5 × 4 = 6 V. (ii) Current through R2 = 2 − 1.5 = 0.5 A; R2 = 6/0.5 = 12 Ω. (iii) p.d. across 2 Ω = 2×2 = 4 V; p.d. across R1 = 12 − 6 − 4 = 2 V; current 2 A ⟹ R1 = 1 Ω.

questions.prompt: Lamps A (12 V, 24 W) and B (6.0 V, current 3.0 A at rated voltage) with resistors R1, R2 are chosen so both lamps operate at rated voltage; supply 15 V. (i) Calculate the current in Lamp A. (ii) State, with reason, the voltmeter reading. (iii) Calculate R2. (iv) Find R1.
questions.msAnswer: (i) I = P/V = 24/12 = 2 A. (ii) Voltmeter = 12 V; A and B are in parallel, so p.d. across each arm = 12 V. (iii) p.d. across R2 = 12 − 6 = 6 V; current = 3 A ⟹ R2 = 2 Ω. (iv) Current through R1 = 3+2 = 5 A; p.d. across R1 = 15 − 12 = 3 V ⟹ R1 = 0.6 Ω.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) (i) State Ohm's Law and write the formula connecting current through a conductor and voltage across it. (ii) In a household, five fans each 100 W run for 4 hours and an electric press 500 W for 2 hours daily. Calculate the cost for 60 days if 1 unit costs Rs. 6.5.
questions.msAnswer: (i) Ohm's law: I ∝ V at constant R; V = IR. (ii) Fans: 5×100×4/1000 = 2 kWh? — per MS: Ef = 0.4 kWh (per fan basis), Ep = 1 kWh; total/day = 1.4 kWh; 60 days = 84 kWh; cost = 84 × 6.5 = Rs. 546.00.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) (i) State Joule's law of heating and write its mathematical expression. (ii) Two resistors 2 Ω and 4 Ω are connected (a) in series (b) in parallel with a battery of given p.d. Compute the ratio of total heat produced in the two cases if total voltage and time are kept the same.
questions.msAnswer: (i) H = I²Rt (H heat in J, I current in A, R resistance in Ω, t time in s). (ii) Rs = 6 Ω; Rp = 4/3 Ω; Hs/Hp = Rp/Rs = (4/3)/6 = 2/9.

questions.prompt: A flow scheme involves substances A, X, Y, Z, M, P, R (chlor-alkali / organic chemistry of sodium chloride and ethanoic acid).

parts.prompt: Derive the names of A, Y, Z, M, P & R.
parts.msAnswer: A = Sodium chloride; Y = Chlorine gas; Z = Hydrogen gas; M = Sodium metal; P = Sodium ethanoate; R = Ethyl ethanoate/ester. (0.5 × 6)

parts.prompt: Improvise an activity to test Z.
parts.msAnswer: Any activity similar to the given figure (testing hydrogen gas — pop sound with a burning splint).

parts.prompt: Name the process in which compounds X, Y & Z are formed from A. Justify your response.
parts.msAnswer: Chlor-alkali process — named for the products formed from chlorine and alkali (sodium hydroxide).

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) Organic chemistry of ethanol and ethanoic acid.

parts.prompt: Distinguish between ethanol and ethanoic acid experimentally.
parts.msAnswer: Ethanoic acid reacts with carbonates/hydrogen carbonates to give salt + CO2 + water (2CH3COOH + Na2CO3 → 2CH3COONa + H2O + CO2); ethanol shows no such reaction.

parts.prompt: Give the IUPAC name of the first member of the alkene formed by addition of conc. sulphuric acid to ethanol. Illustrate with a chemical equation.
parts.msAnswer: Ethene. C2H5OH --(hot conc. H2SO4)--> CH2=CH2 + H2O.

parts.prompt: 'All combustion reactions are oxidation but all oxidation reactions are not combustion.' Justify.
parts.msAnswer: In combustion oxygen is added (so all combustion is oxidation); in oxidation energy may or may not be released, so not all oxidation is combustion.

questions.prompt: Shoots of a plant in shade bend towards sunlight; leaves of 'Touch me not' fold and droop soon after touching. Questions about movements in plants.

parts.prompt: What causes the bending of shoots in the plants (figure A)?
parts.msAnswer: A directional, growth-related response to light. Auxin synthesised at the shoot tip helps cells grow longer; when light comes from one side, auxin diffuses to the shady side, making cells there grow longer so the plant bends towards light.

parts.prompt: What causes the folding of leaves in 'Touch me not' (figure B)?
parts.msAnswer: A growth-independent movement; the plant uses electrical-chemical means to convey information cell to cell. Movement happens at a point different from the touch; cells change shape by changing the amount of water in them (swelling/shrinking).

parts.prompt: Compare the growth of the pollen tube towards the ovule with the movement in part A.
parts.msAnswer: Growth of pollen tube towards the ovule is chemotropism; bending of shoots towards sunlight is phototropism.

parts.prompt: Compare the movement in figure B with the movement of body parts in animals.
parts.msAnswer: Both use electrical-chemical signalling, but plants lack the specialised conducting tissue (nerves) of animals; animal cells change shape via specialised muscle proteins, whereas plant cells change shape by changing their water content.

questions.prompt: A reflecting telescope uses mirrors to gather and focus light to form an image for observation.

parts.prompt: What kind of image of the star is seen by the observer at the eyepiece?
parts.msAnswer: Real image (the final image is formed due to the lens at the eyepiece).

parts.prompt: What kind of mirror is used in this reflecting telescope?
Explain with reason what kind of optical device (type of lens or mirror) is used at the eyepiece.
parts.msAnswer: A converging (convex) lens is used at the eyepiece to collect the rays from the plane mirror and help the viewer see a real erect image of the star.

parts.prompt: What is the role of the plane mirror in the telescope?
parts.msAnswer: The plane mirror laterally inverts the image formed by the curved mirror and directs the rays towards the eyepiece.

questions.prompt: (For Visually Impaired) Azim uses a reading glass (lens) and a scrap of paper to light a fire using the sun's rays.

parts.prompt: Which lens can be used by Azim to create the fire?
What property of the lens helps Azim create the fire?
parts.msAnswer: Its converging property — the lens can converge parallel rays to one point.

parts.prompt: List two more uses of this kind of lens.
parts.msAnswer: To correct hypermetropia; in lenses of telescopes, microscopes and slide projectors.

parts.prompt: Explain with reason the condition under which the lens can form both real as well as virtual images.
parts.msAnswer: If the object is kept between the optical centre and the focus, the image is virtual; in all other cases the image is real.
